originalTitle: Trump must divulge financial details about businesses in $10bn BBC lawsuit
summary: US judge’s ruling could open up window into first family’s multiple ventures, in drawback of lawsuit on media
content: Donald Trump must divulge detailed financial information from his many businesses as part of his $10bn defamation lawsuit against the British Broadcasting Corporation, a federal judge ruled during a discovery hearing on Tuesday, according to news reports. The ruling by US magistrate judge Enjoliqué Lett could open a unique window into the hundreds of businesses owned by Trump’s family trust. It also highlights a potential drawback for Trump’s strategy of bludgeoning critical media with multibillion-dollar lawsuits. Trump filed a defamation lawsuit late last year seeking $10bn in damages over a 2024 documentary called “Trump, A Second Chance,” which features a scene of the speech he made before his supporters attacked the US Capitol on 6 January 2021. The scene splices lines together to make it appear that Trump directly urged his supporters to attack. The BBC apologized to Trump last year for the misleading edit, but has argued the president’s defamation claims have no merit. Lett is also allowing the BBC to subpoena dozens of members of Trump’s inner circle, according to Bloomberg News, in an effort to find evidence of the president’s thoughts and intentions on January 6. Those subpoenas will target Trump family members and key advisers, including Donald Trump Jr, Steve Bannon and Stephen Miller. Trump’s legal team is likely to appeal Tuesday’s decision. Trump’s lawyers are also trying to dodge the forced release of his financial records by limiting the scope of the lawsuit. Alejandro Brito, a Trump lawyer, told Lett on Tuesday that he intends to amend the current complaint, according to Politico. The amended complaint will say the documentary only caused reputational damage, rather than undermining his businesses, making the financial disclosures unnecessary. But for now, the BBC can move forward with requests for Trump’s financial records. The BBC aims to argue that Trump’s wealth skyrocketed since the documentary aired, contradicting the claim that the documentary undermined his businesses. The White House did not immediately return the Guardian’s request for comment.
chineseTitle: 特朗普必须在价值 100 亿美元的 BBC 诉讼中披露有关企业的财务细节
chineseSummary: 美国法官的裁决可能会为第一家庭的多家企业打开窗口，但无需媒体诉讼
chineseContent: 据新闻报道，一名联邦法官在周二的听证会上裁定，作为针对英国广播公司 100 亿美元诽谤诉讼的一部分，唐纳德·特朗普必须披露其众多企业的详细财务信息。美国地方法官 Enjoliqué Lett 的裁决可能会打开一扇独特的窗口，了解特朗普家族信托拥有的数百家企业。它还凸显了特朗普通过数十亿美元的诉讼来打击批评媒体的策略的潜在缺陷。特朗普去年年底对一部名为《特朗普，第二次机会》的 2024 年纪录片提起诽谤诉讼，要求赔偿 100 亿美元，该纪录片展示了他在 2021 年 1 月 6 日支持者袭击美国国会大厦之前发表的演讲场景。该场景将台词拼接在一起，让人觉得特朗普直接敦促他的支持者进行攻击。英国广播公司(BBC)去年就误导性编辑向特朗普道歉，但辩称总统的诽谤指控毫无根据。据彭博社报道，莱特还允许 BBC 传唤特朗普核心圈子的数十名成员，以期找到总统 1 月 6 日想法和意图的证据。这些传票将针对特朗普家族成员和重要顾问，包括小唐纳德·特朗普、史蒂夫·班农和斯蒂芬·米勒。特朗普的法律团队可能会对周二的决定提出上诉。特朗普的律师还试图通过限制诉讼范围来避免被迫公开他的财务记录。据 Politico 报道，特朗普的律师亚历杭德罗·布里托 (Alejandro Brito) 周二告诉莱特，他打算修改当前的投诉。修改后的起诉书将称该纪录片仅造成了声誉损害，而不是损害了他的业务，因此没有必要披露财务信息。但对于
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
